codeDefinitions.name: Hakone – Ashiyu foot bath sand
codeDefinitions.notes: From the volcanic sand at the Hakone open-air foot bath near Gora station.

codeDefinitions.name: Kamakura – Yuigahama beach sand
codeDefinitions.notes: Fine grey-brown sand from Yuigahama beach, collected near the Great Buddha side trail.

codeDefinitions.name: Inokashira Park – pond bank
codeDefinitions.notes: Not really sand — more of a fine grit from the pond bank. Still counts.

codeDefinitions.name: Lawson – Salmon onigiri
codeDefinitions.notes: Lawson's salmon tends to have a slightly sweeter seasoning than 7-Eleven's.

codeDefinitions.name: Kit Kat – Matcha (mini)
codeDefinitions.notes: Bought at Don Quijote. The regional matcha variety, not the standard nationwide one.

codeDefinitions.name: Georgia – European Blend (canned)
codeDefinitions.notes: The vending machine staple. Slightly sweet, slightly wrong, totally correct.

codeDefinitions.name: Ramune – Original lemon
codeDefinitions.notes: Glass bottle with the marble stopper. Bought at Asakusa street stall.

codeDefinitions.name: Kyoto – Indigo furoshiki
codeDefinitions.notes: Indigo-dyed cotton wrapping cloth from a craft shop in Nishiki Market.

codeDefinitions.name: Asakusa – Porcelain maneki-neko
codeDefinitions.notes: Small white lucky cat, gold left paw raised. From the Nakamise shopping street.

codeDefinitions.name: Ghibli Museum entry ticket
codeDefinitions.notes: Purchased online, timed entry 10:00. The ticket is a strip of 35mm film.

itemRecords.label: Hakone volcanic sand – vial #1
itemRecords.notes: The sand has a slightly reddish-brown tint from the volcanic mineral content. Collected from the public foot bath area, not the restricted zones.

itemRecords.label: Kamakura beach sand – vial #1
itemRecords.notes: Collected at low tide. The beach is quite busy but there is a quieter stretch east of the lifeguard station. Vial sealed with wax.

itemRecords.label: Inokashira Park – pond bank grit
itemRecords.notes: Borderline entry for the collection. Very fine, almost silt-like. Still sealed.
First one of the trip. Eaten on the Narita Express.
Post-Senso-ji, eaten on a bench in Asakusa. Somehow better than the first.
Tried Lawson's version for comparison purposes. Research, essentially.
Bought a bag of 15. Brought most home as gifts.
From a vending machine outside Shinjuku Gyoen. Still warm.
Spent way too long figuring out how to push the marble in. Worth it.

itemRecords.label: Indigo furoshiki – medium
itemRecords.notes: 70x70cm. The indigo dye is natural, the shop owner demonstrated three different wrapping styles.

itemRecords.label: Maneki-neko – for the desk
itemRecords.notes: Left paw raised = invites people/luck. Right paw = invites money. Got the people one.

itemRecords.label: Nozomi 17 – Tokyo → Kyoto
itemRecords.notes: Mount Fuji visible from the right side around Shin-Fuji station, roughly 40 minutes in. Clear day, perfect view.

itemRecords.label: Ghibli Museum – 10:00 slot
itemRecords.notes: The ticket itself is a strip of 35mm film from an actual Ghibli production. Mine had a frame from My Neighbour Totoro. No photography inside, but the robot soldier on the rooftop is fair game.
